seo.title: Building Content Generation with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building content generation agents with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables powerful multi-agent workflows powered by Gemini models, handling brand voice enforcement, fact-checking, and editorial guidelines. However, multi-agent delegation often leads to circular dependencies, model inconsistencies, and state corruption, risking brand misalignment and factual errors. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify brand consistency, factual accuracy, originality, tone adherence, and SEO compliance before content reaches production.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Google ADK content generation architecture features a coordinator agent that delegates to specialized sub-agents: a Brand Voice Agent for style enforcement, Research Agent for fact-gathering, Content Writer for drafting, Fact-Checker for validation, and SEO Optimizer for keyword integration. These agents communicate via ADK's session state and tool calling, with Gemini models handling reasoning across the pipeline. Long conversations maintain context through session persistence, but this introduces risks of state corruption and delegation loops.

The workflow begins with a topic input triggering the coordinator, which orchestrates parallel research and voice profiling before sequential drafting and review. Each agent uses ADK's before_agent_callback hooks for instrumentation, making Vex integration seamless. Output flows through final editorial gates ensuring compliance before delivery.

risks.name: Multi-agent delegation creating circular dependencies
risks.description: Coordinator endlessly delegates between Research and Fact-Checker agents due to incomplete tool responses
risks.mitigation: Vex detects delegation loops via session state analysis and enforces max delegation depth

risks.name: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
risks.description: Different Gemini variants (1.5 Flash vs Pro) produce varying brand voice interpretations across agents
risks.mitigation: Vex standardizes outputs against brand voice embeddings before agent handoff

risks.name: Session state corruption in long conversations
risks.description: Extended content generation corrupts ADK session state, causing factual drift in later editorial stages
risks.mitigation: Vex validates session integrity and resets corrupted state with fresh context injection

risks.name: Tool response format mismatches across agents
risks.description: Research Agent's JSON facts incompatible with Fact-Checker's expected schema breaks pipeline
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes tool outputs to consistent schemas before agent consumption

implementation.title: Basic Content Generation Pipeline Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Google ADK coordinator with content generation sub-agents for brand voice and research
implementation.code:
from google_adk import Agent, Coordinator
from vex import VexClient

# Initialize Vex client
vex = VexClient(api_key="vex-your-key")

# Brand Voice Agent
brand_agent = Agent(
    name="brand_voice",
    model="gemini-1.5-pro",
    instructions="Enforce brand voice: conversational, expert, optimistic"
)

# Research Agent
research_agent = Agent(
    name="research",
    model="gemini-1.5-flash",
    tools=["web_search", "fact_lookup"]
)

# Coordinator
coordinator = Coordinator(
    agents=[brand_agent, research_agent],
    before_agent_callback=vex.guardrail_callback
)

result = coordinator.run("Write blog post about AI content generation trends")

implementation.title: Brand Voice Enforcement Agent
implementation.description: Specialized agent that scores content against brand guidelines using embeddings
implementation.code:
from google_adk import Agent
import vex

BRAND_GUIDELINES = """
Tone: Professional yet approachable
Voice: Expert authority with optimism
Length: 800-1200 words
Structure: Problem-Solution-CTA
"""

brand_voice_agent = Agent(
    name="brand_voice_enforcer",
    model="gemini-1.5-pro",
    instructions=f"""
Analyze content against these brand guidelines:
{BRAND_GUIDELINES}

Score 1-10 for:
1. Tone adherence
2. Voice consistency
3. Structure compliance

Respond with JSON: {{"scores": {{...}}, "revision_suggestions": "..."}}
    """
)

# Vex verification before proceeding
@vex.guard("brand_consistency")
def verify_brand_scores(content):
    return content['scores']['tone'] >= 8 and content['scores']['voice'] >= 8

implementation.title: Fact-Checking Pipeline Integration
implementation.description: Research agent gathers facts, fact-checker validates using Vex before content writer proceeds
implementation.code:
from google_adk import Agent, Coordinator

research_agent = Agent(
    name="fact_research",
    tools=["web_search", "wikipedia"],
    instructions="Extract 5 key facts with sources in JSON format"
)

fact_checker = Agent(
    name="fact_checker",
    instructions="Verify each fact against sources. Flag LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH confidence"
)

content_writer = Agent(
    name="content_writer",
    before_agent_callback=lambda x: vex.guard("factual_accuracy", x)
)

pipeline = Coordinator(
    agents=[research_agent, fact_checker, content_writer],
    delegation_rules={
        "research_agent": ["fact_checker"],
        "fact_checker": ["content_writer"]
    }
)

implementation.title: SEO Compliance Checker
implementation.description: Final agent ensures keyword density, readability, and structure before publication
implementation.code:
import re
from google_adk import Agent

def seo_compliance_check(content, keywords, target_density=0.02):
    word_count = len(content.split())
    keyword_count = sum(1 for kw in keywords if re.findall(rf'\b{kw}\b', content, re.IGNORECASE))
    density = keyword_count / word_count if word_count else 0
    return {
        'keyword_density': density,
        'readability_score': len(content.split()) / len(content.split('.')) > 15,
        'h1_present': bool(re.search(r'<h1>', content)),
        'compliant': density >= target_density
    }

seo_agent = Agent(
    name="seo_optimizer",
    instructions="Optimize for SEO: keyword placement, readability, headers",
    before_agent_callback=lambda x: vex.guard("seo_compliance", seo_compliance_check(x, ['content generation', 'AI writing']))
)

vexIntegration.title: before_agent_callback Guardrail Setup
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex verification to every ADK agent execution preventing bad handoffs

vexIntegration.title: Session State Protection
vexIntegration.description: Prevent circular dependencies and state corruption in long conversations

vexIntegration.title: Multi-Verification Content Output
vexIntegration.description: Final verification suite before content publication ensuring all requirements met
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import VexClient, GuardrailSuite

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-key")

content_suite = GuardrailSuite([
    vex.guard("brand_consistency", lambda x: score_brand_voice(x) >= 8.5),
    vex.guard("factual_accuracy", lambda x: fact_confidence(x) > 0.9),
    vex.guard("originality", lambda x: plagiarism_score(x) < 5),
    vex.guard("tone_adherence", lambda x: sentiment_analysis(x)['positivity'] > 0.7),
    vex.guard("seo_compliance", lambda x: seo_score(x) >= 85)
])

# Integrate with ADK final output
final_content_agent = Agent(
    name="editorial_final",
    after_agent_callback=lambda output: content_suite.verify(output)
)

# Usage
content = final_content_agent.run(draft)
if content_suite.passed():
    publish(content)
else:
    print(content_suite.failures())
